from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    single, readable sentence.  The function uses a small set of handcrafted
    templates for the supported predicates and joins the resulting clauses
    with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Basic templates for the most common astronaut predicates.
    # Each template receives (subject, object) and returns a clause string.
    templates = {
        # Personal information
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{s} studied at {o}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was originally named {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{s} retired in {o}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} worked as a {o}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o.strip('\"')}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{s} spent {o} minutes in space",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{s} was selected by NASA in {o}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a {o} citizen",
        # Mission related – subject is astronaut
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a crew member of {o}",
        # Mission related – subject is mission
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} was operated by {o}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"{o} was a backup pilot for {s}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"{o} was the commander of {s}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{o} was a crew member of {s}",
        # Other relations
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{s} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {o}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{s} held the title \"{o}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the {o} ribbon",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{s} was represented by {o}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was also known as {o}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{s} earned {o} awards",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{s} received the award {o}",
        # Fallback
        "default": lambda s, o: f"{s} {o}",
    }

    # Build clauses; keep subject for first clause to allow pronoun later.
    clauses = []
    primary_subject = triples[0].subject
    for i, triple in enumerate(triples):
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, templates["default"])
        clause = tmpl(triple.subject, triple.object)
        # For the first clause, prepend the subject if the template does not already include it.
        if i == 0 and not clause.lower().startswith(primary_subject.lower()):
            clause = f"{primary_subject} {clause}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
